title: Mighty Crunchy Venezuelan Cornmeal Empanadas
description: Salty and crumbly Venezuelan empanadas made with lard and render fat, perfect for a quick snack, and diabetic friendly due to the use of cornmeal which has a lower glycemic index than wheat flour.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, mix together the cornmeal and salt.
Add the lard and render fat to the bowl and mix until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Gradually add the warm water to the bowl, stirring with a fork until the dough comes together.
Turn the dough out onto a floured surface and knead for 5 minutes, until the dough is smooth and pliable.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Roll out each ball of dough into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
Place a tablespoon of shredded cheese in the center of each circle, if using.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a half circle and press the edges together to seal the empanada.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with the beaten egg for a golden glaze.
Place the empanadas in a mason jar or on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 20 minutes, until golden brown.
